$comment: Worked example: New Balance re-expressed as a Prism3 brand schema. Generating this and diffing against the existing hand-built NB token set is the engine regression test (see docs/02-nb-regression-pass.md). Every OKLCH value below is MEASURED from the current NB core-color.json, not guessed — see the regression doc for the conversion. Where a field is the engine's own default (NB never declared it) it is marked $default.

neutralHue.$source: measured: NB neutral is COOL (blue-leaning ~243deg), not warm. Chroma peaks ~0.013 mid-ramp and tapers to <0.004 at the ends.

statusColors.$comment: NB declares success/warning/danger only. There is NO info palette in the source — left null so the engine either omits info or supplies its default. Note: danger shares the brand hue (~23deg); the .ai.json must disambiguate promo-red from error-red.

typography.$source: measured from core-typography.json. NB is NOT a variable-font setup: discrete cuts only.
typography.$comment: Measured step ratios are NOT a single constant: ~1.15 at body sizes (12->14->16), climbing to ~1.25 at display (60->76->96->120). This is the display-pivot pattern the spec predicts (4.2), so the engine needs two ratios, not one.

radius.$source: measured: sm=2 md=4 lg=6 (near-linear, 2px increment) + round=128 (pill). NB is a low-radius / sharp brand.

brand.name: New Balance
brand.voice: Athletic, confident, heritage-modern. Serif display (Garamond), restrained sans UI (Proxima), sharp corners, signature NB Red.
